creator: madpilot@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to yani from comment #2)
> Thanks for your comments. I'm currently using the ".asterisk.makeopts"
> feature to enable the ODBC option, however when there's a new ports tree
> update on the asterisk folder, it removes my customised file. This becomes a
> problem when using poudriere for automated builds and I forget to copy back
> the config file. Anyway, I understand the need to keep the Makefile options
> to the current number.

If you need to customize your ports tree with local files there are at least two solutions.

I personally use ports-mgmt/portshaker, which allows to have an "overlay" in a separate revision control with custom modified ports. It mixes them generating a modified ports tree based on the official one.

Another option, if you're comfortable with git, is to fork the official FreeBSD ports tree on github create a branch where you apply your modifications and import changes from upstream, then use that modified branch in poudriere. You don't need to keep the fork on github, you can have a local fork for this.

Hope these two ideas can be helpful.
